questions.questionText:
From the list of numbers below, write down
(a) a prime number,
(b) a square number,
(c) a multiple of 7.

$$2 \quad 9 \quad 14 \quad 17 \quad 25 \quad 42$$

markScheme.desc: Any prime from the list: 2 or 17
Any square number from the list: 9 or 25
Any multiple of 7 from the list: 14 or 42

questions.explanation: Check each number: prime means exactly two factors; square means it is a perfect square.

questions.questionText: Find the Highest Common Factor (HCF) and Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) of 12 and 18.

markScheme.desc: Correct factor tree or list of factors for both numbers

questions.explanation: HCF uses the lowest power of each shared prime factor; LCM uses the highest power of each prime factor.

questions.questionText:
Write 60 as a product of its prime factors.
Give your answer in index form.

markScheme.desc: Factor tree or repeated division shown

questions.explanation: Divide by the smallest prime at each step until you reach 1.
